manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: marble, octopus, stadium, fish, cover, hospital, microscope, knight, berlin.
Your opponent's team words are: fly, crane, turkey, copper, line, square, cell, tooth.
Distractor words are: england, bell, degree, foot, wake, bill, state.
The kill words are: mammoth.

message.reasoning:
We need provide a single-word clue relating to some of our team's words. Must not be any word from lists or contain parts. Must be semantically related. Provide clue and list of target words that clue relates to (comma separated). We want maximize safe guesses, avoid opponent/distractor/kill. Our words: marble, octopus, stadium, fish, cover (maybe "cover"?), hospital, microscope, knight, berlin. Need a clue relating to some subset.

We must ensure clue not same as any word in any list. Also not contain parts of them. E.g., clue "science" could relate to microscope, hospital? Maybe "science" relates to microscope, hospital (medical science). Might also not refer to opponent. That's fine.

But we must list target words we intend to be guessed. Provide clue "science" with target "microscope, hospital". That seems safe. Check if clue "science" contains parts of any listed word? No. Good.

But maybe we can connect more: "aquatic" could relate to octopus, fish, maybe also marble? Not. "marine". Could be octopus, fish, stadium? No. "marine" not in lists. Could guess octopus, fish. That's 2.

Alternatively "royal" could relate to knight, berlin? Not really. "German" for Berlin, maybe also knight? Not.

Maybe "coliseum" relates to stadium, marble (as material), also maybe? But coliseum is similar to stadium, marble used in coliseum, but perhaps too indirect.

We need safe guess; combine only words that are clearly associated. "hospital" and "microscope" both are medical/health. Also "cover" maybe "blanket"? Not.

"medical" relates to hospital, microscope, maybe also knight? Not.

Thus clue: "medical". Check if "medical" contains parts of any listed words: No. Not same as any. So could target: hospital, microscope. However "medical" also could hint at "cover"? Not. "medical" might lead opponent to guess "cell" (opponent's word) because cell is related to medical. That risk. Maybe "science" also could hint at "cell". But opponent's word cell is in opponent list; we must avoid causing them to guess opponent word. As long as we only list our intended targets, the guessing partner may guess additional words inadvertently, but since we only provide clue and list, the partner will guess only those we list? In the game, the clue is given, and partner guesses words; they might guess incorrectly. But here we just provide clue and list of target words. The system likely will evaluate if any of our targets are opponent/distractor/kill. So we need only list our team words. Provide clue and list.

Thus "science" with targets microscope, hospital. But "science" could also be associated with "microscope" and "hospital", but also "cell", which is opponent. That's okay as long as we don't list cell. The evaluator may consider that the clue is ambiguous but that's okay. The question only asks to produce clue and list. So we can choose.

Maybe better: clue "aquarium" relates to fish, octopus. That's clear. No opponent words. Provide both. Let's choose "aquarium". Check if "aquarium" contains parts of any listed words: no. So:

CLUE: aquarium
TARGETS: fish, octopus

That's two strong. That seems good.

Thus answer
